brand.name: Sandfork
brand.value_prop: Sandfork is a cooking newsletter and journal — recipes, technique, and the people who make food. Published on paper twice a year.

spec.notes:
WHAT WE'RE BUILDING (news_portal): A news organisation's homepage and section pages — different from longform editorial. Density is the characteristic feature: multiple stories per fold, ticker or breaking-news strip, section nav across the top, ads/promotions in the gutter, multiple typographic scales co-existing. This is read-by-everyone, throughout-the-day publication. The aesthetic is news-of-record, formal but high-energy. AVOID: long-form-only treatment, single-hero minimalism. BRAND: Sandfork — "Notes from the cooking class" Sandfork is a cooking newsletter and journal — recipes, technique, and the people who make food. Published on paper twice a year. VISUAL LANGUAGE (editorial_dark): Near-black background (#0E0D0B), warm-cream text (#F4EDE0 primary, #B5AB97 muted). Serif body type ~17-18px, line-height 1.55. Section headers in display serif at heavier weight. Hero photography full-bleed, treated with a dark gradient overlay so headline text reads white on top. Accent: warm amber / honey (palette anchor) for tag chips, links, byline accents. Pull quotes set in italic at oversized weight. NO sidebars; reading flow is top-down. NO sans-serif body, NO bright neon accents. The aesthetic is 'longform after midnight'. DESIGN REFERENCES (style + topic): theguardian.com, nytimes.com, ft.com, nytimes.com night mode, longreads.com, theatavist.com PER-PAGE GUIDANCE: - index: Top: section nav bar + breaking-news ticker. Hero: 1 lead story (large image + huge headline + dek). Below: multi-column news grid — 6-9 secondary stories at varying sizes, mixing image + headline + category tag. Sidebar with most-read list. - video: Video story landing — large player placeholder + transcript / summary below + related video grid. - topic: A topic page (gathering coverage of a story over time). Topic mast, latest stories, 'background' explainer link. - article: An individual news article. Less narrow than longform — measure ~70-75ch. Photo, headline, dek, byline, body, 'read more' related-stories block. - live-blog: A live-blog page. Reverse-chronological updates with timestamps, latest at top, sticky 'live' indicator. - section: A section landing page (e.g. 'Politics', 'Tech'). Section masthead, lead story for the section, then grid of 8-12 stories within the section. IMAGE POLICY (HARD CONSTRAINT): - For purely decorative content (hero illustrations, abstract section dividers, decorative blobs): use NO `` tags. Use CSS gradients, inline SVG, or styled `
` blocks with `background-color` / `aspect-ratio` instead. - For content-essential photography (product photos, dish photos, hotel rooms, portrait avatars, editorial leads), use `` where X is EXCLUSIVELY one of these five filenames: photo-product-1.jpg (generic product / object — warm tones) photo-product-2.jpg (cooler product variant — for grids) photo-portrait-1.jpg (generic person — for avatars / team / bylines) photo-landscape-1.jpg (generic scene — for hero / hospitality leads) illustration-abstract.jpg (mesh-gradient — for splash / decorative hero) - Repeating a filename across multiple `` tags is fine. - ANY OTHER `src=` value will be rejected at validation time. No external URLs, no other filenames, no svg files.
